name: memory_search
description: Search memories with natural language using full-text search. Use this to find relevant past observations, decisions, and context from previous sessions. Returns matching memories with relevance scores. For getting a specific memory by ID, use memory_get instead.

query.description: Natural language search query (e.g., 'bugs in authentication', 'API decisions')

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return

name: memory_get
description: Get a specific memory by its ID. Use this when you have a known memory ID and want full details including input, output, summary, and metadata. For searching memories by content, use memory_search instead.

name: memory_delete
description: Delete a specific memory by its ID. Use this to remove unwanted or outdated observations from the persistent memory database. This action cannot be undone.

name: memory_stats
description: Get database statistics for the persistent memory system. Returns total sessions, observations count, and breakdown by observation type. Use this to understand the size and composition of your memory database. For health check, use memory_health instead.

name: memory_health
description: Check if the persistent memory worker service is running and healthy. Use this to diagnose connectivity issues before performing other memory operations. Returns worker status and version information.

name: memory_timeline
description: Get timeline of observations around a specific memory. Use this to understand the context and sequence of events surrounding a particular memory. Returns neighboring observations in chronological order.

id.description: Memory/observation ID to get timeline around
